executive_summary: Store delivered total sales of 368,436.45 inc VAT (340,018.57 ex VAT) for the week ending 2026-05-16, with an overall store margin of 17.53%.

Total waste of 8,111.35 represents 2.39% of sales ex VAT, which is an elevated figure driven heavily by Deli (1,253.18), Provisions (1,136.11), Produce (1,393.82), and Fish (862.65) collectively accounting for approximately 56% of all waste.

The department P&L total shows a reported loss of -43,606.51 at a margin of -70.22%, which is entirely distorted by a data integrity issue in Cigarettes where an opening stock of 102,500.77 against sales of only 1,284.42 has produced a COS of 101,676.87 and a notional loss of -100,392.45, rendering the department total unreliable this week.

Deli is reporting a genuine operational loss of -2,226.96 at -149.61% margin with no closing stock recorded, a missing stock count that must be investigated and corrected before this figure can be accepted.

Multiple fresh departments are reporting negative or near-zero COS figures (Lamb -30.33, Bacon -201.47, Produce -793.52) indicating stock count discrepancies, likely over-counted closing stock or under-counted purchases, all of which require investigation.

Poultry is the dominant fresh department with sales of 25,390.16, a reported margin of 98.93%, and a scan margin of 16.41%, suggesting the high reported margin is driven by a stock valuation anomaly rather than true trading performance.

Across the fresh departments, scan margins range from 11.62% (Bacon) to 24.47% (Lamb), which represent the more credible margin benchmarks versus the P&L margins where purchase data is absent and stock movements are distorting results.
biggest_wins: Poultry delivered the highest fresh department sales at 25,390.16, contributing a reported profit of 25,119.04, indicating strong volume throughput and effective stock management relative to its small opening and closing stock positions.
Beef posted sales of 12,520.90 with a scan margin of 20.51%, the highest absolute sales volume in the butchery range and a positive indicator of customer demand in this category.

Fish reported sales of 6,083.82 with a scan margin of 19.18% and minimal COS of 33.90, suggesting tight stock control and low shrinkage relative to sales in what is traditionally a high-waste category.

Provisions achieved sales of 4,358.19 at a scan margin of 19.71% with waste of 1,136.11, though the waste figure against this sales level (26.07% of sales) is a concern that offsets the headline performance.
Produce delivered 6,547.11 in sales with a scan margin of 19.13%, indicating solid customer demand in this department despite elevated waste of 1,393.82.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes: Opening stock of 102,500.77 against a closing stock of 823.90 and sales of only 1,284.42 has generated a COS of 101,676.87 and a loss of -100,392.45 at -7,816.16% margin. This is almost certainly a data entry or stock transfer error. An immediate stock count, purchase invoice audit, and system investigation are required before this distorts any period reporting.

Deli: A loss of -2,226.96 at -149.61% margin with no closing stock recorded (null value) means the full opening stock of 3,715.48 has been treated as COS against sales of only 1,488.52. The missing closing stock count must be completed immediately and waste of 1,253.18 in this department this week is already a significant concern independent of the stock issue.

Deli Waste: At 1,253.18, Deli waste is the second highest in the store and at 84.2% of Deli sales of 1,488.52 this represents an unsustainable waste-to-sales ratio that requires immediate review of ordering volumes, production planning, and markdown activity.

Provisions Waste: Waste of 1,136.11 against department sales of 4,358.19 represents 26.1% waste-to-sales, which is critically high. Ordering patterns, rotation discipline, and markdown procedures in this department need urgent review.

Produce: Waste of 1,393.82 is the single highest waste figure in the store. Against sales of 6,547.11 this equates to 21.3% waste-to-sales. Ordering levels, rotation, and markdown activity must be reviewed.

Negative COS Figures: Lamb (-30.33), Bacon & Loose S.R.P (-201.47), and Produce (-793.52) are all showing negative cost of sales, which is not commercially possible and indicates stock count errors, missing goods-in records, or inflated closing stock values. Each of these departments requires a stock count verification and purchase confirmation this week.

Fish Waste: At 862.65, fish waste is the third highest in the store and represents 14.2% of fish sales of 6,083.82, which is above acceptable norms for this category and indicates over-ordering or insufficient markdown activity on short-dated lines.

Overall Department Total Reliability: With purchases recorded as zero across all fresh departments and the Cigarettes anomaly in place, the department P&L total of -43,606.51 is not a meaningful operational figure this week. Management should rely on scan margins as the primary performance indicator until purchase and stock data is corrected.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Opening stock of 102,500.77 is almost certainly erroneous - this must be investigated, corrected, and the department P&L restated before period close. Likely cause is a misallocated goods-in entry or prior-week stock count error.
Beef: Scan margin of 20.51% on sales of 12,520.90 is a solid performance. Waste of 84.07 is well controlled at 0.67% of sales. Monitor pricing and promotional compliance to protect margin.

Lamb: Negative COS of -30.33 indicates closing stock has been over-counted relative to opening. A recount is required. Scan margin of 24.47% is the highest in the butchery range and should be validated for pricing accuracy.
Pork: Scan margin of 20.65% is acceptable. Waste of 81.87 is manageable. COS of 207.81 is the only credible COS figure in the butchery range this week - confirm goods-in receipting is up to date.

Bacon & Loose S.R.P: Negative COS of -201.47 and margin of 117.90% are not credible figures. Closing stock of 1,044.86 exceeds opening stock of 843.39 with no purchases recorded. Verify goods-in receipting and recount closing stock.

Poultry: Dominant sales performer at 25,390.16 but the reported margin of 98.93% is not credible given a COS of only 271.12 against opening stock of 3,004.79. Purchases must be confirmed and receipted. Waste of 202.38 is proportionally low given volume throughput.

Fish: Sales of 6,083.82 with a scan margin of 19.18% is a reasonable commercial outcome. However waste of 862.65 at 14.2% of sales is above target. Review ordering frequency and markdown trigger points for short-dated product.

Produce: Scan margin of 19.13% is in line with expectation. Waste of 1,393.82 is the highest in the store and must be addressed. Negative COS of -793.52 indicates a stock count or receipting issue that requires correction.

Deli: Both the P&L and operational waste position are critical this week. Missing closing stock, a reported loss of -2,226.96, and waste of 1,253.18 require the department manager to complete an immediate stock count and production review.

Provisions: Scan margin of 19.71% is satisfactory but waste of 1,136.11 at 26.1% of sales is the second highest waste-to-sales ratio in the store. Ordering discipline and date rotation must be reviewed urgently.

Bread & Cakes: Waste of 735.63 is noted outside the department P&L breakdown. This level of bakery waste requires review of bake schedules, daily volume planning, and markdown activity on end-of-day surplus.
Dairy: Waste of 379.40 should be monitored. Review rotation and ordering levels, particularly for shorter-dated lines such as fresh milk, yoghurt, and cream.
action_plan: Cigarettes - Immediate: Pull full goods-in receipting history for this department, verify the opening stock figure of 102,500.77, perform a physical stock count today, and escalate to the store accounts team to identify the source of the discrepancy and issue a corrected P&L before the end of this trading week.

Deli - Immediate: Complete a full closing stock count for Deli today, enter the figure into the system, and rerun the department P&L. Review production sheets for the week against recorded waste of 1,253.18 to establish whether over-production or spoilage is the primary driver. Brief the Deli manager on waste-to-sales ratio.

Provisions - This Week: Conduct a full review of Provisions ordering levels and delivery schedules. With waste at 1,136.11 against sales of 4,358.19, ordering volume is clearly exceeding demand. Introduce daily date checks and mandatory markdown of items within two days of expiry.

Produce - This Week: Review Produce ordering quantities for the coming week, reducing lines with consistently high waste. Introduce a daily waste log for the department manager to sign off. Investigate the negative COS of -793.52 by confirming all goods-in purchase orders have been receipted correctly.

Fish - This Week: Review fish ordering par levels and delivery frequency. With waste at 862.65, consider reducing order quantities on slow-moving lines. Ensure a markdown price reduction is applied to all short-dated fish a minimum of four hours before close each day.

Lamb, Bacon, Produce - Stock Count Verification: Arrange a recount of closing stock for Lamb, Bacon & Loose S.R.P, and Produce this week to investigate negative COS figures. Confirm all purchase invoices for the week have been receipted in the system for these departments.

Bread & Cakes - This Week: Review daily bake-off schedules and align production volumes with recent daily sales data. Implement end-of-day markdown activity no later than two hours before store close. Target a reduction in bread and cakes waste from 735.63 toward a 500.00 weekly threshold.

Poultry - Purchases Receipting: Confirm all Poultry purchase invoices for the week have been fully receipted in the system. The reported COS of 271.12 against sales of 25,390.16 is not credible and the department P&L must be restated once receipting is confirmed.

Waste Reporting - Store-Wide: Total waste of 8,111.35 at 2.39% of sales is above the acceptable benchmark. Issue a store-wide waste target communication to all department managers, setting a maximum waste budget of 1.80% of sales for the coming week. Require daily waste figures to be submitted to the store manager.

Scan Margin Monitoring: In the absence of reliable purchase data and with multiple departments showing anomalous P&L margins, use scan margins as the primary weekly management KPI. Post scan margins for all departments on the manager noticeboard and review at the weekly team briefing.

Department P&L Data Integrity: Raise a formal query with the accounts and buying team regarding the absence of purchase data across all fresh departments. Without goods-in receipting, the department P&L is not fit for purpose. Establish a deadline for all purchase invoices to be receipted within 48 hours of delivery.

Dairy - Monitor: Place dairy on a weekly watch for waste. At 379.40 this week, introduce rotation checks at every delivery and confirm first-in first-out discipline is being followed by all staff members handling ambient and chilled dairy lines.
